title: A wave of dissent sweeps through Servant of the People, Zelensky's party
subhed: As Ukraine prepares to pass a series of laws in order to secure international funding, the president appears to have lost some of his influence over his parliamentary majority. His loyal supporters are trying to keep his party cohesive, as it's deeply connected to his rise to power.

title: 'Historic day': Artemis II astronauts travel further into space than ever before

In Hungary, unease grows over Viktor Orban's crony capitalism
subhed: Companies that have thrived on public contracts since the Fidesz party came to power in 2010 have seen their share prices tumble ahead of the April 12 legislative elections, as polls indicate that opposition candidate Péter Magyar could win.

title: The French Tennis Federation's goal to renovate obsolete clay courts
subhed: The organization hopes to double the proportion of courts with this surface across the country by 2032. Nearly 13,000 porous concrete courts could be renovated and covered with artificial clay, which requires little watering and maintenance.

title: With 'Normes Corps' at the Palais de Tokyo, vulnerable bodies unite their strength
subhed: For its new season, the Paris contemporary art center is presenting six solo exhibitions by artists exploring fragility, disability and difference.

title: Are Trump's ideologues intellectuals or imposters?
subhed: Curtis Yarvin has become a leading figure in the Neo-Reactionary movement (also known as the Dark Enlightenment), which has now made it to the White House. But can this blogger really be considered an intellectual? Political theorist Arnaud Miranda and sociologist Olivier Alexandre debate the origins and roles of one of the thinkers who has Trump's ear.

Looking for Cécile, a 'bad girl' in France's sinister former 'preservation schools'
subhed: Until the mid-20th century, correctional facilities run by the French prison administration held minors accused of theft or 'debauchery' in appalling conditions. Upon his retirement, Michel Bey discovered that his mother, Cécile, who died when he was 5, was one of them.

title: How Morocco is renewing its influence networks in France
subhed: Some of Morocco's traditional networks of influence in France have been put on hold in recent years. How they might be restructured remains uncertain, while new voices have struggled to replace high-profile figures.

title: China's execution of a Frenchman convicted of drug trafficking casts a chill on relations with Paris
subhed: Chan Thao Phoumy was sentenced in 2010 after authorities dismantled a methamphetamine trafficking network. France has condemned the fact that his defense was not allowed to attend his final court hearing.
